skill.name: self-improving-agent
skill.description: A universal self-improving agent that learns from ALL skill experiences. Uses multi-memory architecture (semantic + episodic + working) to continuously evolve the codebase. Auto-triggers on skill completion/error with hooks-based self-correction.
skill.summary: A Claude Code skill that learns from every interaction to continuously improve itself using multi-memory architecture.
skill.author: charon-fan

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate Claude Code skill for self-improvement. The static scanner flagged 121 potential issues, but after semantic evaluation, all are false positives. External command detections are markdown backticks in documentation. Network detections are academic reference URLs. Filesystem detections are standard Claude Code config paths. No actual security risks present.

content.value_statement: Claude Code lacks built-in learning from experience. This skill adds multi-memory architecture so your AI continuously improves by extracting patterns from every task.
actual_capabilities: Extracts reusable patterns from every skill interaction
Stores experiences in semantic, episodic, and working memory
Auto-corrects skill guidance when errors occur
Updates related skills based on learned patterns
Tracks confidence levels for learned patterns
limitations: Requires Claude Code hooks integration for full functionality
Pattern extraction quality depends on user feedback
Cannot learn from skills outside the agent-playbook ecosystem
Self-correction only triggers on error or completion events

use_cases.title: Developer Using Multiple Skills Daily
use_cases.description: A developer who uses prd-planner, debugger, and code-reviewer daily. The skill extracts patterns across these interactions to improve each skill's guidance.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers using Claude Code for daily coding tasks

use_cases.title: Team Lead Building Code Quality
use_cases.description: A team lead wants to improve all skills in their agent-playbook. The skill identifies gaps and suggests improvements based on real usage.
use_cases.target_user: Team leads managing AI-assisted development workflows

use_cases.title: AI Engineer Customizing Skills
use_cases.description: An AI engineer wants to build a personalized learning system. The skill provides templates and architecture for continuous improvement.
use_cases.target_user: AI engineers building custom Claude Code workflows

prompt_templates.title: Basic Self-Improvement Trigger
prompt_templates.prompt: 自我进化 - analyze what happened in this session and extract any patterns we can learn from.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after any skill completes to trigger pattern extraction

prompt_templates.title: Error-Focused Learning
prompt_templates.prompt: What went wrong in this debugging session? Extract the root cause and add it to the debugger skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after encountering a bug that the debugger skill could have caught

prompt_templates.title: Pattern Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the patterns in memory/semantic-patterns.json. Which ones have high confidence? Which need more validation?

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Skill Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze how prd-planner and code-reviewer could share patterns. What guidance appears in both?

output_examples.input: 自我进化 - analyze our session
output: 1. **state_monitoring_over_callbacks**: Use usePrevious for state-driven side effects
Rate this pattern (1-10): Were the updates helpful?

output_examples.input: What went wrong with the PRD we created?
output: UI/UX specs lacked exact positioning details

best_practices: Extract patterns at the right abstraction level - not too specific, not too general
Gather user feedback to validate pattern quality before broad application
Use evolution markers to track the source of every improvement
Validate guidance changes before applying them broadly
anti_patterns: Over-generalizing from a single experience - wait for 3+ occurrences
Ignoring negative user feedback - it indicates real issues
Creating contradictory patterns across different skills

faq.question: Does this skill work without Claude Code hooks?
faq.answer: Yes, but with limited functionality. Without hooks, you must manually trigger self-improvement with phrases like '自我进化' or 'self-improve'.

faq.question: How does this store memory?
faq.answer: It uses three memory types: semantic (patterns/rules), episodic (specific experiences), and working (current session context). All stored as JSON files.

faq.question: Can it learn from any skill?
faq.answer: Yes, it can learn from any skill in the agent-playbook. The skill defines an evolution priority matrix for different skill types.

faq.question: What happens to incorrect patterns?
faq.answer: Self-correction triggers on errors. It verifies root cause, applies corrections with markers, and validates fixes before broad application.

faq.question: How is confidence tracked?
faq.answer: Each pattern has a confidence score (0-1) based on application count and user feedback. Higher confidence patterns are applied more broadly.

faq.question: Is this safe to use in production?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill modifies skill files based on learned patterns but uses human-in-the-loop validation. All changes include evolution markers for traceability.
